reasoning: All three reach the same verdict (decline as offered, counter with a scoped/roadmap-aligned deal), so depth of reasoning decides it. A is strongest: it names the attribute-substitution trap, explicitly invokes two competing reference classes and identifies generalizability as the hinge, runs a premortem, flags escalation-of-commitment to a services model, attaches an explicit 65% confidence, and lists the three specific numbers it would verify. B is close and well-structured (clean burn math, explicit case-for/case-against, concrete counter-proposal) but does not quantify its confidence or surface its own cognitive traps as deliberately. C does the core math and reaches a sound restructured-terms list, but is the thinnest on bias-awareness and alternative reference classes and offers the least calibrated framing of its uncertainty.
